author: akashchi
title: benchmark_app crashes with STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION on Windows in test_benchmark_app_no_warmup_with_api_modes[async-CPU-Python]
signature: benchmark_app returned non-zero exit status 3221225477|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: Samples
failed_jobs.symptom: benchmark_app process crashes with exit code 3221225477 (STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION) in async-CPU-Python test

key_errors: subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '('benchmark_app', '-i', ..., '-d', 'CPU', '-niter', '1', '-nireq', '1', '-api', 'async', '-no_warmup')' returned non-zero exit status 3221225477.
root_cause: benchmark_app crashes with Windows STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION (0xC0000005) when running in async API mode with no warmup on CPU. This is a known flaky crash in the Windows benchmark_app sample test unrelated to the PR change (which modifies CI/GHA workflow files only). The identical failure was observed in run 29257338803 (PR #36843) just hours earlier, confirming this is a pre-existing intermittent issue.
reproduction_steps: Run: benchmark_app -i <image> -m bvlcalexnet-12.onnx -d CPU -niter 1 -nireq 1 -api async -no_warmup on a Windows runner
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed Samples job as this is a known transient crash
Investigate and fix the underlying access violation in benchmark_app async path on Windows
Consider adding a skip/xfail marker for this specific test variant on Windows until root cause is fixed
prevention_strategies: Track the recurring Windows benchmark_app STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION pattern and fix the root cause in the async inference path to prevent continued flakiness.
historical_context: Same signature (hash 51203fa3d66f0e88) seen previously in run 29257338803 (PR #36843) at 2026-07-13T16:41:56Z. This is the second occurrence today.
